payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields are: token counts (input, output, total), provider and model IDs, estimated price in USD, and latency percentiles p50, p90, p95.
payload.decision: The required fields for evaluation accounting are token counts (input/output/total), provider and model identifiers, estimated price in USD, and latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) to enable cost-performance analysis and reproducibility.

entity_resolution.reason: All entities are explicitly defined in the context and require no external resolution.

memory_cards.statement: Token counts (input, output, total) are required for eval accounting to measure computational usage and enable cost attribution.
Provider and model IDs are required to uniquely identify the service and model version used in evaluation for reproducibility and cost tracking.
Estimated price in USD is required for eval accounting to quantify monetary cost and enable cost-performance tradeoff analysis.
Latency percentiles (p50, p90, p95) are required to characterize response time distribution and support performance benchmarking in eval accounting.

details: All required accounting fields explained with supporting memory cards.
Output adheres to schema and constraints.
